data.title: REG - Skillcast Group PLC - Investor Presentation via Investor Meet Company
data.description:
RNS Number : 0897A Skillcast Group PLC 19 September 2025  

Skillcast Group PLC

("Skillcast", the "Group" or the "Company")

Investor Presentation via Investor Meet Company

Skillcast Group PLC is pleased to announce that Vivek Dodd (CEO) and Richard Steele (CFO) will provide a live webcast to discuss the Company's H1 2025 results via the Investor Meet Company platform on 23 September 2025 at 14.00 BST.

The presentation is open to all existing and potential shareholders. Questions can be submitted pre-event via the Investor Meet Company dashboard up until 22 Sept 2025, 09:00 BST, or at any time during the live presentation.

Investors can sign up to Investor Meet Company for free and add to meet Skillcast Group PLC via:

https://www.investormeetcompany.com/skillcast-group-plc/register-investor

Investors who already follow Skillcast Group PLC on the Investor Meet Company platform will automatically be invited.

Enquiries:

Skillcast Group plc

+44 (0)20 7929 5000

Richard Amos, Chairman

Vivek Dodd, Chief Executive Officer

Richard Steele, Chief Financial Officer

Cavendish (Nominated Adviser & Broker)

+44 (0)20 7220 0500

Jonny Franklin-Adams / Isaac Hooper (Corporate Finance)

Sunila de Silva (Corporate broking)

About Skillcast Group plc

Skillcast helps companies build ethical, inclusive, and resilient workplaces. It provides content and technology to digitise and streamline compliance processes and manage them from a single compliance portal. Its product range includes a Learning management system with comprehensive off-the-shelf compliance course libraries, a Policy hub, Staff declarations, Anonymous surveys, CPD tracking, and compliance registers for gifts, expenses, PA dealing, whistleblowing.

data.title: Euro Could Rise to $1.24 By End of 2026, RBC Says — Market Talk
data.description: The euro could rise to $1.24 in the fourth quarter of 2026 from $1.1759 currently as the European Central Bank should leave interest rates unchanged for a prolonged period, RBC Capital Markets strategists say. The market continues to price a risk of a further rate cut, but this could be squeezed out in the coming months as the growth outlook holds up, they say. In contrast, the Federal Reserve restarted rate cuts and signaled further reductions on Wednesday. Lower rates will make it cheaper for foreign investors to buy protection against the risk of the dollar falling. Meanwhile, ongoing asset rotation out of the U.S. to Europe is expected. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Citi sees Brent crude at $60 by year-end as OPEC+ ramps up production
data.description:
Citi analysts on Friday forecast Brent crude oil prices would fall to $60 per barrel by year-end and average $62 per barrel between the second and fourth quarters of 2026, citing OPEC+ production increases and China's stockpiling.

The bank revised its global liquids balance outlook after OPEC+ announced plans to unwind an additional 1.6 million barrels per day (mb/d) of voluntary cuts starting in October 2025.

Citi said that could lead to stock builds of 1.1 mb/d in 2025 and 2.1 mb/d in 2026, adding slack to an already-loosening global supply.

By end-2026, Citi estimates global liquids inventories could climb to 10.9 billion barrels, equivalent to 103 days of forward demand cover.

Under a bear case scenario, Citi assigned a 30% probability of Brent prices falling below $60 per barrel, potentially to $50, on weaker global demand, faster growth in non-OPEC supply, and lower compliance among OPEC+ members.

Its bullish scenario, with a 10% probability, could push Brent prices above $75 on increased geopolitical disruption.

Global oil demand is expected to grow by 0.7 mb/d in 2025 and 1 mb/d in 2026, though trade disputes could trim diesel consumption by as much as 0.3 mb/d. Citi reaffirmed its Brent target of $60 per barrel over the next 6 to 12 months.

Brent crude futures were trading at $66.93 a barrel by 1024 GMT while U.S. West Texas Intermediate futures at $62.92.

data.title: DexTech Medical AB, Annual Report 2024-07-01 - 2025-06-30
data.description:
DexTech

The Board of Directors and the CEO of Dextech Medical AB (DexTech), org.nr. 556664-6203, with its registered office in Stockholm, hereby submits the Annual Report for the financial year 2024-07-01 - 2025-06-30.

For more information about DexTech, please contact:

Gösta Lundgren - CFO

DexTech Medical AB

Phone: +46 (0) 707104788

E-mail: gosta.lundgren@dextechmedical.com

DexTech Medical AB is a Swedish research company that, based on its technology platform, has developed four drug candidates that are protected by patents. The lead candidate is OsteoDex for the treatment of castration-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC) with bone metastases. A successful clinical phase II study has been conducted with OsteoDex where the results show high tolerability with mild side effects and treatment effect on patients who fail existing drugs. DexTech's goal is to out-license each drug candidate no later than after completion of the phase II study. DexTech Medical AB is listed on the Spotlight Stock Market.

data.title: Bank of Japan to Start Offloading More Assets — WSJ
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO — The Bank of Japan said it would start to offload its huge portfolio of exchange-traded funds, a sign of growing confidence in the economy despite challenges including President Trump's tariffs.

The multiyear program of purchases "was a necessary component of the large-scale monetary easing policy implemented to achieve the 2% inflation target," Gov. Kazuo Ueda said at a news conference Friday. But now, with the economy improving and markets working smoothly, "there was no longer a need to continue the measure," he said.

The central bank on Friday said it would offload about 330 billion yen, or $2.23 billion, of its ETF holdings annually. The BOJ bought ETFs under its aggressive monetary-easing program aimed at tackling years of deflation.

At that pace, Ueda said it would take more than 100 years to completely unwind the BOJ's $251 billion ETF portfolio, underlining the central bank's cautious pace so as to minimize market ripples.

"This marks a significant and symbolic step away from the ultraloose policies of the Abenomics era," said StoneX senior market analyst Matt Simpson, referring to pro-growth policies pushed by former Prime Minister Shinzo Abe, including cheap money and robust government spending.

Japanese stocks fell following the decision, with the Nikkei Stock Average declining as much as 1.8% before closing 0.6% lower. The index has recently set record highs on expectations of new political leadership at home and easing concerns over the U.S. economic outlook.

Japan's ruling Liberal Democratic Party is searching for a new leader after Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba announced his resignation this month. One front-runner, Sanae Takaichi, is a fan of bold stimulus for Japan's economy, helping fuel the stock rally.

The BOJ kept its policy rate steady at 0.5%, where it has been since its January rate increase, as it assesses U.S. tariffs and domestic political uncertainty. While a trade agreement between the U.S. and Japan cleared some doubts, the economic and corporate impact of tariffs remains unclear.

Although Japan secured a lower tariff rate than Washington had initially threatened, companies still face higher costs than before, which could erode competitiveness in the U.S. market and hurt profits.

The BOJ is watching whether companies maintain capital investment and pay raises under the new tariff regime. Key indicators include a closely watched corporate sentiment survey and the quarterly BOJ branch managers meeting, which tracks regional economic conditions.

Some economists expect the BOJ to hold off on rate increases until next year, while others say it can make a move as soon as October if it becomes confident that prices and wages are on track to keep growing at a healthy pace.

Two BOJ policy board members, Hajime Takata and Naoki Tamura, dissented from Friday's decision to hold, signaling they believe the central bank is ready for another increase.

In judging the timing of the next rate increase, Ueda said the BOJ would be looking at how much the downside risks from U.S. tariffs will affect the Japanese economy and prices, and whether food inflation will ease.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: Top of the Street: Stellantis, Kuehne und Nagel, H&M, European aerospace and defence
data.description:
A round-up of notable broker activity this morning from Europe's top-ranked* analysts:

** Berenberg upgrades Stellantis to "buy" from "hold", citing an improving narrative thanks to better U.S. inventory and upcoming product momentum

** Deutsche Bank cuts Kuehne und Nagel to "hold" from "buy" saying the logistics group will face challenges in H2 and forecasting a material step down in profits in Q3 in a challenging backdrop

** Inderes cuts H&M to "sell" from "reduce", pointing to weak short-term growth drivers and citing revenue growth and the uncertain environment as ongoing concerns

INITIATIONS AND REINSTATEMENTS

** Goldman Sachs starts aerospace and defence stocks BAE Systems and Rheinmetall at "buy", citing cross-domain business and cash conversion for the former and alignment to Europe's rearmament and Germany's spending for the latter

** It starts Thales at "sell" due to sales decline in its cyber & digital business and uncertainty around its civil telecoms space unit, it also starts Dassault Aviation at "sell", staying cautious on its business jet outlook and French politics

** It initiates Leonardo at "neutral" on similar valuation to peers and European defence opportunities despite lower Italian spending

** The brokerage initiates at "buy" aerospace companies Airbus on its straightening supply chain, Rolls-Royce on stronger cash generation in civil aeronautics and expertise in high-growth end-markets, Melrose Industries saying it is undervalued, and Safran on underestimated civil aftermarket growth

** It starts MTU Aero at "neutral" given high valuation compared to peers

(*Analyst rankings from Thomson Reuters StarMine. The scale is from 1-star to 5-star with 5 being the best. Analysts are ranked on earnings accuracy as well as relative performance of recommendations over trailing 12-month & 24-month periods.)

data.title: Middle East Crude-Benchmarks extend decline; Mercuria steps up sales
data.description:
Middle East crude benchmarks Oman and Dubai extended decline on Friday amid abundant supply of November-loading cargoes while several trading firms, led by Mercuria, stepped up sales on the Platts window.

Mercuria and Sinochem delivered one cargo each of Upper Zakum crude to Vitol following the deals on the window.

This marked the 24th cargo delivered by Mercuria so far this month.

"It's definitely weakening," a refining source said, referring to the benchmarks, adding that there are many spot cargoes being offered.

Meanwhile, freight rates for Very Large Crude Carriers soared to their highest in more than two years, according to industry sources and LSEG data, as tanker supply tightens with a rise in Middle East exports and more arbitrage supplies to Asia.

Chinese and Indian refiners are expected to ramp up runs in the coming months ahead of the Golden Week and Diwali periods, supporting long-haul Atlantic-to-Pacific trades, Vortexa freight analyst Wanying Zhang said, adding that voyage counts are already exceeding seasonal norms.

"While current levels, near three-year highs, may ease somewhat, the combined effect of seasonal Asian demand, reduced fleet availability, and long-haul tonne-mile expansion provides a firm underlying floor for VLCC earnings into the fourth quarter," Zhang said.

SINGAPORE CASH DEALS

Cash Dubai's premium to swaps fell 34 cents to $2.62 a barrel.

Indian Oil Corp has bought 2 million barrels of U.S. West Texas Intermediate Midland crude from Totsa for November delivery via a tender, trade sources said.

The WTI cargo was sold at about $3.50 a barrel above dated Brent on delivery at port basis to Vadinar, one of the sources said.

Separately, Hindustan Petroleum Corp bought 2 million barrels of West African crude for end-October delivery via a tender, trade sources said.

The cargo of 1 million barrels each of Nigerian Bonny Light and Angolan Pazflor was sold by Shell , they said.

Prices were not immediately available.

NEWS

Russia has increased its planned oil loadings from the Baltic port of Ust-Luga and the Black Sea port of Novorossiisk for September, following disruptions at its main western outlet, Primorsk, two sources familiar with the schedule told Reuters.

Kuwait's Oil Minister Tariq Al-Roumi said on Thursday he anticipated higher oil demand following this week's U.S. interest rate cut, particularly from Asian markets.

Santos boss Kevin Gallagher said on Friday he has no plans to retire and expects a sharp jump in cash flow in the next few years to boost the Australian gas producer's shares, after its third suitor in seven years scrapped a takeover bid.
